headline: Neighbors Rescue Grandmother from Burning Home
meta_description: Quick-thinking neighbors pulled an elderly woman with Alzheimer's from her burning home after spotting flames, acting as heroes in moments of crisis.
summary_short: Brave neighbors rushed into a burning house and saved an 87-year-old woman, acting immediately when they spotted danger.
body:
Phyllis Day was asleep in her Leicestershire home when fire broke out in the utility room. Fortunately, alert neighbors spotted flames and smoke billowing from the house and sprang into action without hesitation. They rushed inside the burning building and brought the 87-year-old woman to safety, even as her daughter watched the terrifying scene unfold on her doorbell camera from a distance.

Day, who lives with Alzheimer's, was unable to help herself escape. The neighbors' swift response and courage made the difference between tragedy and rescue. Their actions remind us that community bonds and quick thinking can save lives, and that everyday people often become heroes in moments that matter most.
